title: Rook and Pawn vs Rook
summary: The most common endgame at every level

steps.title: Half Wins, Half Draws
steps.text: The most frequent endgame is R+P vs R. About half of cases are drawn, half are wins — and the result often hinges on knowing Philidor (draw) or Lucena (win) by heart.

steps.title: Key Rules of Thumb
steps.text: Rule 1: The defender's rook belongs BEHIND the passed pawn (yours or theirs). Rule 2: Rook pawn (a/h) is drawish — the defending king often reaches the corner. Rule 3: The attacker must push the pawn while keeping his king escorted.
